items.title: From sequence to scale: Gene editing’s new era in biologics manufacturing
items.snippet: In this episode of Denatured, you’ll hear from Jack Crawford, CEO of Demeetra, and Magnus Gustavsson, chief commercial officer at NorthX Biologics. We unpack the evolution of cell line development — CHO cells, targeted integration, transposases and the collaboration models speeding biologics from sequence to GMP.

items.title: AAIC 2026: Tau-targeted Alzheimer’s treatments heat up while amyloid therapies persist
items.snippet: The lineup at the Alzheimer’s Association International Conference will provide critical insight into where the industry is headed with regard to targets being explored to vanquish the elusive neurodegenerative disease.

items.title: GSK and Hansoh’s ADC clinches late-stage win, teeing up regulatory submission in China
items.snippet: GSK and Hansoh Pharmaceutical’s antibody-drug conjugate success validates their partnership, one of the many deals in which Big Pharma has tapped a China company for promising cancer candidates.

items.title: AstraZeneca, Ionis’ Wainua woes send waves across ATTR-CM space
items.snippet: The failure of AstraZeneca and Ionis’ Wainua in a late-stage study of ATTR-CM casts doubt on Alnylam’s next-generation candidate but is good news for others in the space, including BridgeBio and Intellia Therapeutics.

items.title: Roche scraps 2 Huntington’s studies, handing Ionis more bad news
items.snippet: Roche’s decision to discontinue the Ionis-partnered trials came soon after the biotech sustained a late-stage failure in ATTR-CM.

items.title: Amgen continues to defend Tavneos as global regulatory pressure mounts
items.snippet: Regulators on both sides of the Atlantic are pushing for the withdrawal of the rare disease treatment that accounted for just 1% of Amgen’s 2025 revenue. Nevertheless, Amgen continues to defend the medicine, which was acquired in the $3.7 billion buyout of ChemoCentryx.

items.title: Biopharma R&D needs ‘structural redesign’ to maximize AI impact: report
items.snippet: Biopharma companies won’t fully capture the benefits of AI unless they reorganize their R&D units, according to McKinsey.

items.title: Cancer, rare disease dominate FDA approvals in H1 2026 as AstraZeneca, J&J clean up
items.snippet: The FDA greenlit 26 novel therapies in the first half of 2026, including four for cancer and six for orphan indications. Meanwhile, AstraZeneca and Johnson and Johnson took home a combined 11 of the agency’s 79 total approvals, including supplemental nods.

items.title: Psychedelics surge again as Compass, Definium provide more clinical validation
items.snippet: Psychedelics are gaining momentum in depression, with one treating physician predicting that the drug class could “wipe out the SSRIs” if safety and durability hold up.

items.title: Saol boards FDA’s resubmission boat, armed with new survival data
items.snippet: Saol Therapeutics is the latest biotech to resubmit for approval of a drug rejected under former FDA Commissioner Marty Makary, following REGENXBIO and Replimune.

items.title: AstraZeneca, Ionis slip as ATTR-CM drug fails Phase 3
items.snippet: The late-stage miss is “surprising,” Stifel analysts said, given that Wainua’s mechanism of silencing transthyretin protein expression has previously proven effective.

items.title: GSK abandons Alector after back-to-back neuro stumbles
items.snippet: GSK and Alector first partnered in 2021 to advance two antibodies for neurodegenerative diseases. Both assets have since failed to show significant clinical benefit.

items.title: FDA freezes CRL trove as ‘radical transparency’ push hits speedbump
items.snippet: An unnamed pharma filed a citizen petition in April seeking reforms to the way the FDA publicly releases rejection letters, alleging that the policy “contravenes decades of agency practice.”

items.title: Tanabe pens deal to sell plants to Towa in post-buyout pivot
items.snippet: After being bought by Bain for $3.3 billion, Tanabe has reached a deal to sell its manufacturing unit and 17 products.

items.title: Through flux and fluidity, FDA keeps coming back to rare disease
items.snippet: Even as FDA approvals for biologic therapies fell in the first half of 2026, regulatory experts are optimistic about a turnaround in the rare disease space after the departure of key leaders at the agency. Still, there will continue to be tension between science and politics.

items.title: Amgen recalls almost 1 million bottles of heart failure drug over quality issue
items.snippet: The discovery of a foreign substance prompted Amgen to voluntarily recall batches of the medicine Corlanor made in Italy.

items.title: Novartis cuts New Jersey workforce again, ousting 322 employees
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: Obesity newcomer Kalohexis makes confidential IPO bid 3 months after spinoff
items.snippet: Kalohexis is working on peptide therapies, including one for obesity that could offer an alternative approach to the GLP-1s that currently dominate the weight loss space.

items.title: AstraZeneca bets up to $1.9B in respiratory disease collab with Sino Biopharmaceutical
items.snippet: In AstraZeneca’s third trip to Asia this year, the pharma secured ex-China rights to a dual inhibitor of PDE3 and PDE4, which in a Phase 2b study significantly improved lung function and lowered symptom burden in patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disorder.

items.title: Vertex carries M&A uptick into H2, IPOs and VC raises also grow, FDA on pace despite turmoil
items.snippet: A surprising deal from Vertex Pharmaceuticals adds to Big Pharma’s acquisitive streak as Crinetics folds into the cystic fibrosis drugmaker. Meanwhile, IPOs and venture capital raises trend upward, but mostly for derisked companies. Plus, FDA decisions slow only slightly as the hunt for a permanent leader drags on.

items.title: Biotech’s China problem: An issue of national security or economic value for the US?
items.snippet: Congressional letters sent to the CEOs of Eli Lilly, Pfizer, Merck, BMS and AbbVie this week voicing concerns about the pharmas' clinical trials in China highlight an ongoing discrepancy in how government and industry think about the rise of the Asian country’s biotech industry.

items.title: BIO 2026: Cell and gene therapy sector must break the mold to thrive
items.snippet: If cell and gene therapy makers are going to achieve their mission to improve patients’ lives, the industry must come together to share information across stakeholders, from regulators to manufacturers to payers.

items.title: At FDA, ‘caretaker mode’ is better than power trip mode
items.snippet: UniQure’s planned third-quarter submission for its Huntington’s disease gene therapy may be a harbinger of a more flexible FDA under acting commissioner Kyle Diamantas—but how long will it last? And how can companies be sure these positive decisions won’t just be reversed?

items.title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
items.snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.
